summary: McKenzie recounts losing track of what day it is after a grueling month of 12-16 hour workdays culminating in a major presentation. The presentation went well and resulted in a functional spam filter that will be open-sourced. He reflects on how this experience highlights the appeal of self-employment, where such extreme work schedules wouldn't be necessary. He notes he still likes his day job despite the exhaustion.
key_points: A month of 12-16 hour days caused McKenzie to lose track of what day of the week it was
The intense work period produced a functional spam filter soon to be released as open source
The experience reinforced his desire for self-employment as an escape from unsustainable work schedules
money_quotes: You know what I really like about the prospect of self-employment? No months of 12 to 16 hour days, and no forgetting what day it is.
key_insight: Grueling day-job schedules that cause you to lose track of time reinforce the appeal of self-employment and working on your own terms.
